Material risks (from 10-K Item 1A): for further discussion of government action with respect to greenhouse gas and climate change and the associated risks to Chevron’s business.
The following disclosures reflect our beliefs and opinions as to factors that could materially and adversely affect us in the future.

References to past events are provided by way of example only and are not intended to be a complete listing or a representation as to whether or not such factors have occurred in the past or their likelihood of occurring in the future. BUSINESS AND OPERATIONAL RISK FACTORS Chevron is exposed to the effects of changing commodity prices Chevron is primarily in a commodities business that has a history of price volatility.
An investment in the company carries significant exposure to fluctuations in global prices of crude oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids.

Extended periods of low prices or demand for crude oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids have had, and in the future can have, a material adverse impact on the company’s results of operations, financial condition and liquidity.
Among other things, the company’s upstream earnings, cash flows, and capital expenditure programs could be negatively affected, as could its production and proved reserves.

Downstream earnings could be negatively affected because they depend upon the supply and demand for refined products and the associated margins on refined product sales. A significant or sustained decline in liquidity could adversely affect the company’s credit ratings, potentially increase financing costs and reduce access to capital markets.

The company may be unable to realize anticipated cost savings, expenditure reductions and asset sales that are intended to compensate for such downturns, and such downturns may also slow the pace and scale at which we are able to invest in our business, including our New Energies businesses.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $48.61B in FY2026 Q1, -4.1% YoY — contracting trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $45.73B (-5.9% vs current Q) — set by 25 broker contributors.
Next earnings: 2026-08-11 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
Watch FCF burn: $-1.55B last quarter — pressures buyback / dividend coverage near-term.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $191B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $183B → FY2 $196B (+7.0% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 14.0% — average operating profile over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: -4.0% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Energy): Commodity-cycle exposure + capital discipline — long-cycle return-on-capital story.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.
